# Example Output: GSD-Ready Implementation Plan

*This is an example of the execution plans generated by the VibePM Architect, ready to be digested by the `get-shit-done` CLI or other execution agents.*

---

# PROJECT.md

## 1. Vision
A simple YouTube summarizing web app that processes videos completely asynchronously and provides a podcast playback dashboard.

## 2. Tech Stack (Locked)
- **Framework**: Next.js 14 App Router
- **Language**: TypeScript (strict mode)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS + Shadcn UI
- **Database/Auth**: Supabase (Postgres)
- **External**: OpenAI (Whisper), ElevenLabs (TTS)

## 3. Architecture Constraints
- Must use Server Components for data fetching. Only use `"use client"` when interactivity (like audio playback) is strictly required.
- Do NOT build custom UI components. Exclusively use Shadcn.
- Async Pipeline: Web browser -> API Route -> Queue -> Processing -> Database Update. No long-polling on the server.

---

# ROADMAP.md

## Phase 1: Foundation (Database & Auth)
Set up the core schema required for generation tracking and Supabase Auth.

## Phase 2: Core Dashboard (UI Shell)
Build the protected routes, sidebar, and the URL input form.

## Phase 3: AI Async Pipeline (The Mover)
Build the background worker to download YouTube, transcribe with Whisper, and convert to TTS.

## Phase 4: Final Presentation (Playback)
Add live status polling on the dashboard and embed the audio player.

---

# PLAN.md (Phase 1 example)

*This file can be directly fed into the execution agent to spawn parallel waves.*

## Wave 1 (Parallel execution possible)

<task type="auto">
  <name>Initialize Next.js & Shadcn UI</name>
  <files>tailwind.config.ts, components/ui/*, app/layout.tsx</files>
  <action>
    Initialize a standard Next.js 14 project using App Router and TypeScript. 
    Install Tailwind CSS and initialize Shadcn UI via its CLI.
    Create a basic unauthenticated landing page at `app/page.tsx` with a welcoming Hero section.
  </action>
  <verify>Run `npm run build` and ensure the landing page renders without errors.</verify>
  <done>Next.js initialized with Shadcn components accessible.</done>
</task>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Supabase Client Setup</name>
  <files>utils/supabase/server.ts, utils/supabase/client.ts, .env.local.example</files>
  <action>
    Install the `@supabase/ssr` library.
    Build standard Supabase server/browser clients following official Next.js App Router guidelines.
    Create an `.env.local.example` file exposing the needed keys.
  </action>
  <verify>TypeScript compilation passes and `server.ts` correctly reads cookies.</verify>
  <done>Supabase client ready for injection into middleware and components.</done>
</task>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Database Migration SQL (Schema)</name>
  <files>supabase/migrations/00001_init.sql</files>
  <action>
    Generate a `.sql` file that contains:
    1. An enum `generation_status` ('pending', 'completed', 'failed').
    2. A `generations` table with: `id` (uuid), `user_id` (refs auth.users), `youtube_url` (text), `status`, `audio_url` (text).
    3. RLS policies allowing users to read and insert their own records.
  </action>
  <verify>Ensure valid PostgreSQL syntax.</verify>
  <done>SQL file created and ready for manual or automated application to Supabase.</done>
</task>

## Wave 2 (Sequential: Depends on Wave 1)

<task type="auto">
  <name>Supabase Auth Middleware & Pages</name>
  <files>middleware.ts, app/login/page.tsx</files>
  <action>
    Create a Login and Signup page at `/login` using Supabase Auth UI or native form.
    Implement a `middleware.ts` that protects all routes under `/(dashboard)`. If the session is invalid, redirect to `/login`.
  </action>
  <verify>Accessing `/dashboard` without login yields a 307 redirect to `/login`.</verify>
  <done>Authentication barrier fully operational.</done>
</task>
